```erlang
-module(word_counter).

solve(Chunks, Queries) ->
    S = lists:concat(Chunks),

    % 1. Extract potential segments: maximal contiguous sequences of [a-z-]
    PotentialSegments = extract_potential_segments(S),

    % 2. Identify valid words and count occurrences
    WordCounts = identify_and_count_words(PotentialSegments),

    % 3. Build the result array
    Ans = lists:map(fun(Query) ->
        case maps:find(Query, WordCounts) of
            {ok, Count} -> Count;
            error -> 0
        end
    end, Queries),

    Ans.

%% Helper: Extract maximal contiguous sequences of [a-z-]
extract_potential_segments(S) ->
    extract_segments(S, 0, [], []).

extract_segments([], _, CurrentSegment, Segments) ->
    lists:append(Segments, [CurrentSegment]) when CurrentSegment /= "".

extract_segments([C|Rest], StartIndex, CurrentSegment, Segments) ->
    case is_word_char(C) of
        true ->
            NewSegment = [C | CurrentSegment],
            extract_segments(Rest, StartIndex + 1, NewSegment, Segments);
        false ->
            % Separator encountered. Finish current segment if it exists.
            if CurrentSegment /= [] ->
                Segments' = [lists:reverse(CurrentSegment) | Segments],
                extract_segments(Rest, StartIndex + 1, [], Segments')
            else
                extract_segments(Rest, StartIndex + 1, [], Segments)
            end
    end.

%% Helper: Check if a character is a word character (a-z or -)
is_word_char(C) ->
    case C of
        Char when char_code(Char) >= 97 and char_code(Char) <= 122 ->
            true; % a-z
        '-' ->
            true;
        _ ->
            false
    end.

%% Helper: Identify valid words and count occurrences
identify_and_count_words(Segments) ->
    foldl(process_segment, #{}, Segments).

process_segment(WordCounts, Segment) ->
    case is_valid_word(Segment) of
        true ->
            maps:update_count(Segment, WordCounts);
        false ->
            WordCounts
    end.

%% Helper: Check if a segment adheres to the strict word definition
is_valid_word(Segment) ->
    check_hyphen_rule(Segment, 0).

check_hyphen_rule(Segment, Index) when Index >= length(Segment) ->
    true.
check_hyphen_rule(Segment, Index) ->
    case Segment !! Index of
        '-' ->
            % Check boundaries for the hyphen
            if Index == 0 or Index == length(Segment) - 1 ->
                false; % Hyphen at start or end
            else
                % Check surrounding characters
                case Segment !! (Index - 1) of
                    Char1 when char_code(Char1) >= 97 and char_code(Char1) <= 122 ->
                        case Segment !! (Index + 1) of
                            Char2 when char_code(Char2) >= 97 and char_code(Char2) <= 122 ->
                                check_hyphen_rule(Segment, Index + 1);
                            _ ->
                                false
                        end;
                    _ ->
                        false % Previous char is not a letter
                end
            end;
        Char when char_code(Char) >= 97 and char_code(Char) <= 122 ->
            check_hyphen_rule(Segment, Index + 1);
        _ ->
            % This case should ideally not be reached if segments were extracted correctly, 
            % but it implies an invalid character exists.
            false
    end.

%% Map utility to increment count
maps:update_count(Key, Map) ->
    case maps:find(Key, Map) of
        {ok, Count} -> maps:put(Key, Count + 1, Map);
        error -> maps:put(Key, 1, Map)
    end.

    % 1. Extract potential segments: maximal contiguous sequences of [a-z-]
    PotentialSegments = extract_potential_segments(S),

    % 2. Identify valid words and count occurrences
    WordCounts = identify_and_count_words(PotentialSegments),

    % 3. Build the result array
    Ans = lists:map(fun(Query) ->
        case maps:find(Query, WordCounts) of
            {ok, Count} -> Count;
            error -> 0
        end
    end, Queries),

    Ans.

%% Helper: Extract maximal contiguous sequences of [a-z-]
extract_potential_segments(S) ->
    extract_segments(S, 0, [], []).

extract_segments([], _, CurrentSegment, Segments) ->
    lists:append(Segments, [CurrentSegment]) when CurrentSegment /= "".

extract_segments([C|Rest], StartIndex, CurrentSegment, Segments) ->
    case is_word_char(C) of
        true ->
            NewSegment = [C | CurrentSegment],
            extract_segments(Rest, StartIndex + 1, NewSegment, Segments);
        false ->
            % Separator encountered. Finish current segment if it exists.
            if CurrentSegment /= [] ->
                Segments' = [lists:reverse(CurrentSegment) | Segments],
                extract_segments(Rest, StartIndex + 1, [], Segments')
            else
                extract_segments(Rest, StartIndex + 1, [], Segments)
            end
    end.

%% Helper: Check if a character is a word character (a-z or -)
is_word_char(C) ->
    case C of
        Char when char_code(Char) >= 97 and char_code(Char) <= 122 ->
            true; % a-z
        '-' ->
            true;
        _ ->
            false
    end.

%% Helper: Identify valid words and count occurrences
identify_and_count_words(Segments) ->
    foldl(process_segment, #{}, Segments).

process_segment(WordCounts, Segment) ->
    case is_valid_word(Segment) of
        true ->
            maps:update_count(Segment, WordCounts);
        false ->
            WordCounts
    end.

%% Helper: Check if a segment adheres to the strict word definition
is_valid_word(Segment) ->
    check_hyphen_rule(Segment, 0).

check_hyphen_rule(Segment, Index) when Index >= length(Segment) ->
    true.
check_hyphen_rule(Segment, Index) ->
    case Segment !! Index of
        '-' ->
            % Check boundaries for the hyphen
            if Index == 0 or Index == length(Segment) - 1 ->
                false; % Hyphen at start or end
            else
                % Check surrounding characters
                case Segment !! (Index - 1) of
                    Char1 when char_code(Char1) >= 97 and char_code(Char1) <= 122 ->
                        case Segment !! (Index + 1) of
                            Char2 when char_code(Char2) >= 97 and char_code(Char2) <= 122 ->

                        end;

        _ ->

            % but it implies an invalid character exists.
            false
    end.

    case maps:find(Key, Map) of
        {ok, Count} -> maps:put(Key, Count + 1, Map);
        error -> maps:put(Key, 1, Map)
    end.

-module(word_counter).

%% Helper function to check if a segment adheres to the word definition.
%% A word is a maximal contiguous sequence of [a-z-] such that every '-'
%% is surrounded by [a-z].
is_valid_word(Segment) when is_list(Segment) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
        case Char of
            'a'..'z' -> Acc;
            '-' ->
                case Acc of
                    {Prev, Next} when Prev = 'a'..'z' and Next = 'a'..'z' -> Acc;
                    _ -> {false, false}
                end;
            _ -> {false, false} % Should not happen if segment is pre-filtered
        end
    end, {true, true}, Segment)
    % This manual fold is overly complex for Erlang's structure here.
    % Let's use pattern matching and list processing instead for clarity on the rule.
    ; true.

is_valid_word_internal(Segment) when is_list(Segment) ->
    lists:all(fun(I) ->
        case lists:nth(I, Segment) of
            '-' ->
                case {lists:nth(I-1, Segment), lists:nth(I+1, Segment)} of
                    {Prev, Next} when Prev = 'a'..'z' and Next = 'a'..'z' -> true;
                    _ -> false
                end;
            'a'..'z' -> true;
            _ -> false % Should not happen
        end
    end, lists:seq(0, length(Segment) - 1)).

%% The main solver function.
%% solve(chunks, queries) -> list(integer).
solve(Chunks, Queries) ->
    % 1. Concatenate chunks to form s.
    S_list = lists:flatten(Chunks),
    S = lists:map(fun(C) -> C end, S_list), % Assuming Chunks elements are lists of chars (strings)
    S_str = lists:concat(S), % This might need adjustment depending on how Erlang handles list of strings from input

    % Since the input type is list of strings (list(str)), and Erlang uses binary or list of char
    % strings, we must assume the input strings are processed character by character or concatenated
    % into a single structure we can iterate over. Let's assume concatenation into a list of chars.
    S_chars = lists:flatten(Chunks),

    % 2. Identify all valid "words" in s and count their occurrences.
    % We extract maximal contiguous segments of [a-z-].
    
    extract_segments(S_chars, Segments) =:= Segments,

    WordCounts = maps:new(),

    lists:foreach(fun(Segment) ->
        if is_valid_word_internal(Segment) ->
            % Since the problem guarantees queries are valid words, we count all valid segments.
            case maps:get(Segment, WordCounts) of
                {count, C} -> maps:put(WordCounts, Segment, {count, C + 1});
                no -> maps:put(WordCounts, Segment, {count, 1})
            end
        end
    end, Segments),

    % 3. Build the result array.
    Ans = [
        case maps:lookup(Query, WordCounts) of
            {_, C} -> C;
            no -> 0
        end
        || Query <- Queries
    ],
    Ans.

%% Helper to extract maximal segments of [a-z-] from a list of characters.
extract_segments(Chars, Segments) ->
    extract_segments_acc(Chars, [], []).

extract_segments_acc([], CurrentSegment, AccumulatedSegments) ->
    if lists:length(CurrentSegment) > 0 ->
        [CurrentSegment | AccumulatedSegments];
    true ->
        AccumulatedSegments.

extract_segments_acc([Head|Tail], CurrentSegment, AccumulatedSegments) ->
    case Head of
        'a'..'z' ->
            extract_segments_acc(Tail, [Head|CurrentSegment], AccumulatedSegments);
        '-' ->
            extract_segments_acc(Tail, [Head|CurrentSegment], AccumulatedSegments);
        _ ->

            if lists:length(CurrentSegment) > 0 ->
                NewSegments = [CurrentSegment | AccumulatedSegments];
            true ->

%% A word is a maximal contiguous sequence of [a-z-] such that every '-'
%% is surrounded by [a-z].
is_valid_word(Segment) when is_list(Segment) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, Char) ->
        case Char of
            'a'..'z' -> Acc;
            '-' ->
                case Acc of
                    {Prev, Next} when Prev = 'a'..'z' and Next = 'a'..'z' -> Acc;
                    _ -> {false, false}
                end;
            _ -> {false, false} % Should not happen if segment is pre-filtered
        end
    end, {true, true}, Segment)
    % This manual fold is overly complex for Erlang's structure here.
    % Let's use pattern matching and list processing instead for clarity on the rule.
    ; true.

    lists:all(fun(I) ->
        case lists:nth(I, Segment) of
            '-' ->
                case {lists:nth(I-1, Segment), lists:nth(I+1, Segment)} of
                    {Prev, Next} when Prev = 'a'..'z' and Next = 'a'..'z' -> true;
                    _ -> false
                end;
            'a'..'z' -> true;
            _ -> false % Should not happen
        end
    end, lists:seq(0, length(Segment) - 1)).

    S_chars = lists:flatten(Chunks),

    % We extract maximal contiguous segments of [a-z-].

            case maps:get(Segment, WordCounts) of

        end
    end, Segments),

            {_, C} -> C;
            no -> 0

    ],
